content.text: Users are reporting that the last item in an array is never processed. I need to find the bug in this codebase. Please search for loops or array iteration code that might have an off-by-one error — for example, code using `< array.length - 1` instead of `< array.length`, or `i <= array.length - 2`, or similar patterns. Also look for any test files that might describe this behavior. Report back: 1) the file(s) and line numbers where the bug likely is, 2) the exact code, 3) any relevant test files.

## Bug Found

I've identified the off-by-one error in your codebase. Here's the detailed report:

### 1) File and Line Number
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775461910678-mpjjqp/src/utils.ts`
**Line:** 7

### 2) Exact Buggy Code
```typescript
for (let i = 0; i < items.length - 1; i++) {
  results.push(items[i].toUpperCase());
}
```

**The Problem:** The loop condition `i < items.length - 1` causes it to stop one iteration early. When the array has `n` elements (indices 0 to n-1), this loop only processes items at indices 0 through n-2, skipping the last element at index n-1.

**The Fix:** Should be `i < items.length` instead.

### 3) Relevant Test File
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775461910678-mpjjqp/tests/utils.test.ts`

The test file actually describes the expected behavior that is currently failing:
- Line 5-11: Test `"should process all items in the array"` expects `processItems(["hello", "world", "foo"])` to return `["HELLO", "WORLD", "FOO"]`, but the current code will skip "FOO"
- Line 17-19: Test `"should handle two items"` expects both items to be processed, which will currently fail since it only processes the first item

The comment in the source file (line 3) even acknowledges this: *"Known issue: users report the last item is never processed."*

content.text:
## Bug Found

I've identified the off-by-one error in your codebase. Here's the detailed report:

### 1) File and Line Number
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775461910678-mpjjqp/src/utils.ts`
**Line:** 7

### 2) Exact Buggy Code
```typescript
for (let i = 0; i < items.length - 1; i++) {
  results.push(items[i].toUpperCase());
}
```

**The Problem:** The loop condition `i < items.length - 1` causes it to stop one iteration early. When the array has `n` elements (indices 0 to n-1), this loop only processes items at indices 0 through n-2, skipping the last element at index n-1.

**The Fix:** Should be `i < items.length` instead.

### 3) Relevant Test File
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775461910678-mpjjqp/tests/utils.test.ts`

The test file actually describes the expected behavior that is currently failing:
- Line 5-11: Test `"should process all items in the array"` expects `processItems(["hello", "world", "foo"])` to return `["HELLO", "WORLD", "FOO"]`, but the current code will skip "FOO"
- Line 17-19: Test `"should handle two items"` expects both items to be processed, which will currently fail since it only processes the first item

The comment in the source file (line 3) even acknowledges this: *"Known issue: users report the last item is never processed."*

content.text:
All 6 tests pass. Here's a summary of what was fixed:

**Bug:** Off-by-one error in `src/utils.ts` — the loop condition `i < items.length - 1` caused the loop to stop one element short, skipping the last item in every array.

**Fix:** Changed the condition to `i < items.length` so all elements, including the last one, are processed.

```diff
- for (let i = 0; i < items.length - 1; i++) {
+ for (let i = 0; i < items.length; i++) {
```
